data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Lower Amid Global Energy Concerns
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened on a softer note on Friday, with all major indices in the red as surging global energy prices and geopolitical tensions weighed on investor sentiment.

The benchmark FBM KLCI slipped 3.78 points or 0.22% to 1,709.42. The broader market indexes also declined, with the FBM70 down 86.86 points (-0.51%) to 17,100.62, the F4GBM retreating 2.95 points (-0.29%) to 1,006.51, the FBMEMAS falling 38.93 points (-0.31%) to 12,478.40 and the FBMSHA easing 23.83 points (-0.20%) to 12,149.91.

Among the most active stocks, AAX led the decliners, down 0.12 at 1.30, while Hengyuan bucked the trend with a gain of 0.05 to 1.64. Other active movers included CapitalA (0.445, -0.01), VS (0.305, -0.01) and Armada (0.345, unchanged). Top gainers at the open included Nestle (108.80, +0.70), HL Bank (23.46, +0.48) and Petronas Chemicals (3.60, +0.09).

The market is following a weak lead from Wall Street, where the Dow tumbled 784.67 points or 1.61%, the NASDAQ dropped 58.50 points (-0.26%) and the S&P 500 lost 38.79 points (-0.56%) amid concerns that sharply higher crude prices could impact economic growth and inflation, as reported by RTTNews.

Crude oil prices continued their surge on Thursday, with West Texas Intermediate spiking 8.7% to US$81.17 a barrel, as the Middle East conflict disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts have warned that any prolonged disruption could keep energy markets volatile and weigh further on equities.

Investors are expected to monitor the broader energy situation closely, with the STI and wider market likely to remain under pressure at the start of trading.

data.description:

data.title: Crypto Turns Choppy As Bitcoin Eases, Ethereum Stabilises
data.description:
The crypto market turned choppy on March 6, with Bitcoin retreating after a strong rally while Ethereum held relatively firm around the US$2,000 mark.

Bitcoin slipped to about US$70,900, down roughly 2.5% on the day after briefly trading above US$72,000 earlier in the week, as traders locked in profits following a recent surge fuelled by renewed institutional demand and haven buying.

The world’s largest cryptocurrency had climbed nearly 20% from February lows near US$60,000, with inflows into spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds helping push prices back above the US$70,000 threshold.

Meanwhile, Ethereum traded around US$2,085, largely steady over the past 24 hours as investors continued to test the psychological US$2,000 support level, a key battleground for market sentiment in recent sessions.

Analysts say the latest price swings reflect a mix of profit-taking, macro uncertainty and geopolitical tensions, which have driven investors toward alternative assets while amplifying volatility across digital markets.

Despite the pullback, the broader crypto market remains resilient, with Bitcoin still trading near multi-week highs and Ethereum maintaining its recovery range as investors watch for fresh institutional flows and macro catalysts to determine the next directional move.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Nearly Flat Against US Dollar Amid Cautious FX Sentiment
data.description:
The ringgit was nearly flat against the US dollar at Friday’s open as foreign exchange sentiment stayed cautious amid no signs of the Middle East crisis easing over the past week, as reported by Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency traded at 3.9430/9550 against the greenback, compared with Thursday’s close of 3.9415/9480.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said risk-off sentiment continued to dominate the market, with major indices in the red while oil prices remained elevated, reflecting a cautious trading environment.

On the domestic front, Mohd Afzanizam said Bank Negara Malaysia’s decision on the overnight policy rate (OPR) yesterday was in line with economists’ expectations, adding that policymakers appear to be monitoring developments in the Middle East before making any changes to the current monetary policy stance.

“Hence, the ringgit is expected to remain range-bound today,” he said to Bernama.

At the time of writing, Brent crude traded at US$85.41 per barrel, while the yield on the 10-year US Treasury note rose four basis points to 4.13 per cent.

Yesterday, the ringgit closed 0.04 per cent lower against the US dollar at RM3.9447, after moving within a narrow range of RM3.9305 to RM3.9483.

At the open, the ringgit was mostly higher against major currencies.

The local currency strengthened against the euro to 4.5762/5902 from 4.5808/5884 at Thursday’s close and improved vis-à-vis the Japanese yen to 2.5024/5102 from 2.5080/5124 previously.

However, it weakened against the British pound to 5.2659/2819 from 5.2635/2722 yesterday.

The ringgit traded mixed against its ASEAN peers.

It appreciated vis-à-vis the Thai baht to 12.4072/4539 from 12.4711/5016 on Thursday and strengthened against the Singapore dollar to 3.0795/0891 from 3.0885/0938 yesterday.

It was little changed against the Indonesian rupiah at 233.2/234.0 from 233.1/233.6 at the previous close and was unchanged versus the Philippine peso at 6.72/6.75 compared with 6.72/6.74.

data.title: FBM KLCI Edges Up At Midday Despite Decliners Dominate Trading
data.description:
Shares on Bursa Malaysia traded mixed at midday, with the benchmark index edging higher despite broad selling pressure across the market.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 4.19 points to 1,717.39, supported by gains in selected heavyweights, while market breadth remained negative with 686 decliners outpacing 291 gainers and 448 counters unchanged.

Broader indices were mixed, with the FBM EMAS Index inching up 0.31 of a point to 12,517.64, while the FBM ACE slid 119.58 points to 4,376.88.

Nevertheless, trading activity remained brisk, with 1.99 billion shares changing hands valued at RM1.76 billion.

Among the most actives, AirAsia X Bhd slipped 14 sen to RM1.28 after heavy trading of over 93.9 million shares, while Lotte Chemical Titan Holding Bhd surged 13 sen to 47 sen and Velesto Energy Bhd added one sen to 33 sen.

On the blue-chip leaderboard, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd topped the gainers list, jumping 69 sen to RM4.20, followed by MISC Bhd, which climbed 56 sen to RM8.80.

Plantation heavyweight Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd rose 30 sen to RM19.02, while Petronas Gas Bhd and PPB Group Bhd each gained 18 sen to RM18.16 and RM11.08, respectively.

On the downside, Nestlé (M) Bhd led decliners, falling 90 sen to RM107.20, followed by Allianz Malaysia Bhd, which dropped 48 sen to RM20.90.

Other notable losers included Hong Leong Industries Bhd, down 38 sen to RM17.20, and Malayan Cement Bhd, which slipped 28 sen to RM8.05.

data.title: KLCI Ends Higher At 1,718 As Blue Chips Lift Market
data.description:
The Malaysian benchmark index closed slightly higher on March 6, with buying in selected blue chips lifting the market despite weakness in smaller-cap counters.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 4.86 points, or 0.28%, to 1,718.06, after trading between 1,708.48 and 1,719.95 during the session.

The performance in the broader market, however, was mixed, with the FBM EMAS Index edged up 10.03 points to 12,527.36, while the FBM ACE slid 96.43 points to 4,400.03, reflecting pressure on smaller-cap and technology-related counters.

Market participation remained active with 3.72 billion shares worth RM4.05 billion changing hands.

Among the most actively traded counters, AirAsia X Bhd fell 14 sen to RM1.28 on heavy volume of 166.3 million shares, while Lotte Chemical Titan Holding Bhd jumped 14.5 sen to 48.5 sen amid brisk trading.

Oil and gas player Velesto Energy Bhd gained one sen to 33 sen, while Capital A Bhd slipped 2.5 sen to 43 sen.

Market observers noted that selective bargain hunting in index-linked stocks helped support the benchmark, even as investors remained cautious in the broader market.

data.title: Wall Street Slides As Weak Jobs Data, Oil Price Spike Rattle Markets
data.description:
US stocks ended sharply lower on March 6 as a weak jobs report and a surge in oil prices rattled investors, raising fears of slowing economic growth and renewed inflation pressure.

Reuters reported that the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.95% to 47,501.55, posting its steepest weekly drop since early April 2025. The S&P 500 slid 1.33% to 6,740.00, marking its worst week since mid-October, while the Nasdaq Composite declined 1.59% to 22,387.68. The Russell 2000 also logged its sharpest weekly fall since early August.

Investor sentiment deteriorated after data showed the US unemployment rate rising to 4.4%, heightening concerns that the labour market may be cooling just as geopolitical tensions drive energy prices higher.

Oil prices surged after the US and Israel launched military strikes in Iran, disrupting shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and fuelling fears of tighter supply. US crude futures jumped more than 12% to above US$90 per barrel, while Brent crude rose about 8.5% to US$92. Qatar also warned that prices could climb to as high as US$150 per barrel.

The spike in energy costs stoked worries about higher input costs for companies and a potential delay in interest-rate cuts by the Federal Reserve System, as inflation risks resurface.

Banking stocks were among the hardest hit. The S&P 500 Banks Index dropped 2.03% amid concerns that higher rates and weaker credit conditions could pressure lenders.

Asset-management giant BlackRock tumbled 7.1% after limiting withdrawals from a major private credit fund. Western Alliance Bancorporation fell 8.4% after filing a lawsuit against Jefferies Financial Group over unpaid loans linked to bankrupt auto-parts supplier First Brands Group, sending Jefferies shares down 13.5%.

Travel stocks also lagged as fuel costs climbed, with the S&P Passenger Airlines Sub-Index dropping 4.07%. Energy shares edged up 0.13% on expectations of stronger revenue from higher oil prices.

Among individual movers, chipmaker Marvell Technology surged 18.4% after projecting fiscal 2028 revenue above analysts’ estimates.

Trading volume on US exchanges reached 19.95 billion shares, well above the 17.82 billion average over the past 20 sessions.

data.title: Singapore Stocks Open Lower Amid Middle East Tensions And Rising Oil Prices
data.description:
Singapore shares opened under pressure on Friday, as the Straits Times Index (STI) fell 33.98 points, or 0.7%, to 4,812.58 in early trading, with 36 gainers against 151 decliners on volume of 92.14 million shares worth S$160.81 million.

Market watchers attributed the softer start to lingering concerns over soaring energy prices following the Middle East conflict, as well as weak leads from Wall Street, where the Dow Jones tumbled 784.67 points, or 1.61%, and the S&P 500 lost 0.56% on Thursday (RTTNews, 5 Mar 2026).

The STI has alternated between gains and losses in recent sessions, coming off a three-day slide that saw it shed almost 80 points or 1.7% (RTTNews, 5 Mar 2026).

On Thursday, the index had managed a modest 33.81-point gain, closing at 4,846.56, supported by financials, property stocks, and industrial issues.

Among notable movers, Singapore Technologies Engineering soared 4.43%, UOL Group climbed 3.47%, while Yangzijiang Shipbuilding fell 1.40% and Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation declined 0.29%.

Regional derivatives also reflected caution, with MSCI Singapore Index Futures last trading at 440.10, while Nikkei 225 and FTSE China A50 futures showed mixed movements.

In commodities, West Texas Intermediate crude for April delivery jumped 8.7% to US$81.17 per barrel on Thursday amid heightened geopolitical risk and fears of disruption through the Strait of Hormuz, marking a 21.1% increase for the week, according to a commentary by RTTNews.

Major Singapore-listed counters opened cautiously, with DBS down 0.181% to S$55.02, Singtel up 1.22% to S$4.91, and UOB rising 0.50% to S$36.00.

Investors continue to weigh global energy market volatility alongside domestic corporate fundamentals.

The early session underlines expectations of renewed consolidation for the Singapore market, with analysts monitoring oil prices and Middle East developments closely for clues on potential market direction.

data.title: South Korea’s Kospi Ends Nearly Flat After Volatile Week
data.description:
South Korean stocks finished almost unchanged on March 6, as the Kospi rebounded from earlier losses to close at 5,584.87, up 0.02%, despite ongoing geopolitical and energy price pressures.

The benchmark index swung sharply during the session after opening lower amid continued concerns over the Middle East conflict and surging oil costs, but buying by retail investors helped trim losses and lift the market by the close.

Auto makers and defence stocks outpaced broader gains, with Hyundai Motor and Hanwha Aerospace among the bright spots, while heavyweight tech names like Samsung Electronics and SK hynix lagged.

The local won weakened against the US dollar, reflecting broader risk aversion. Trade volume remained robust as the market capped a turbulent week marked by sharp sell‑offs and previous rebound attempts.

Investors now watch for further catalysts as volatility persists amid global uncertainty.

data.title: Market Closes Lower With Decliners Outpacing Gainers
data.description:
The stock market ended the day on a softer note, with decliners outnumbering gainers, reflecting cautious market sentiment at the close.

At the closing bell, 675 counters declined versus 411 counters advanced and 518 were unchanged on the local bourse.

Among the top losers, consumer heavyweight Nestlé (M) Bhd slid RM2.30 to RM105.80. Semiconductor player Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd fell 94 sen to RM30.10, while Allianz Malaysia Bhd shed 52 sen to RM20.86.

Industrial counters also weakened, with Hong Leong Industries Bhd down 40 sen to RM17.18 and Malayan Cement Bhd losing 39 sen to RM7.94.

On the gainers, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd surged 78 sen to RM4.29, leading the market’s upside with strong trading activity of 76.6 million shares changing hand. Plantation heavyweight Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd rose 64 sen to RM19.36, while MISC Bhd climbed 46 sen to RM8.70.

Also among the gainers were United Plantations Bhd, which added 28 sen to RM30.28, and PPB Group Bhd, up 24 sen to RM11.14.

Despite selective buying in plantation and energy-linked stocks, broader market sentiment remained subdued, with selling pressure outweighing gains across the exchange.

data.title: Gainers Edge Losers As Trading Volume Eases
data.description:
Market breadth on Bursa Malaysia turned positive on March 5, with gainers outpacing losers 572 to 511, although overall trading activity moderated from the previous session.

A total of 523 counters ended unchanged, while 1,016 were untraded and 11 suspended. Market turnover slipped to 2.85 billion shares worth RM3.26 billion compared to 3.51 billion shares valued at RM3.79 billion on March 4.

Leading the gainers list was Nestlé (M) Bhd, which climbed RM1.10 to RM108.10, followed by Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd up 54 sen to RM31.04. Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd added 25 sen to RM7.65, while Malayan Cement Bhd rose 21 sen to RM8.33 and Hong Leong Industries Bhd gained 20 sen to RM17.58.

On the downside, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd led the losers, sliding 90 sen to RM33. Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd fell 42 sen to RM20.84, while AirAsia X Bhd dropped 26 sen to RM1.42. Kelington Group Bhd lost 20 sen to RM4.91 and United Plantations Bhd eased 20 sen to RM30.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Rebounds As Bargain Hunting Lifts KLCI To 1,713
data.description:
FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI ended higher on March 5 as bargain hunting emerged after the benchmark briefly slipped below the psychological 1,700 level in recent sessions, tracking the positive tone across regional markets.

At the close, the FBM KLCI rose 14.98 points, or 0.88%, to an intraday high of 1,713.20 from March 4’s close of 1,698.22.

Broader market indices also finished in positive territory, with the FBM Emas Index gaining 82.86 points to 12,517.33, while the FBM Shariah Index advanced 85.50 points to 12,173.74. The FBM 70 Index edged up 33.22 points to 17,187.48.

Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd gained four sen to RM11.70, Public Bank Bhd rose six sen to RM4.90 and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd climbed nine sen to RM8.04. RHB Bank Bhd closed unchanged at RM8.50, while Hong Leong Bank Bhd slipped eight sen to RM22.98.

Trading activity was led by AirAsia X Bhd, Capital A Bhd, Bumi Armada Bhd, Tan Chong Motor Holdings Bhd and Zetrix AI Bhd, which were among the most actively traded counters of the day.

data.title: US Dollar Eyes Biggest Weekly Gain In A Year As Middle East Tensions Mount
data.description:
The US dollar held broadly steady in early Asian trade Friday, poised for its largest weekly gain in over a year as the intensifying conflict in the Middle East boosted demand for safe-haven assets.

The dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of currencies, was down slightly by 0.06% at 99.00 but is on track for a 1.4% weekly gain, the steepest since November 2024. The euro was little changed at US$1.1612, the yen edged up 0.06% to 157.5 per dollar and sterling was marginally higher at US$1.3361.

“The Middle Eastern conflict continues at its current intensity, it’s likely to bring sustained higher inflation, a stronger US dollar and a vastly reduced chance of Fed rate cuts,” said IG market analyst Tony Sycamore.

Crude prices have surged again amid the war, driving inflation concerns in energy-dependent economies and altering expectations for global central bank policies. West Texas Intermediate spiked 8.7% on Thursday to US$81.17 a barrel, marking a 21.1% jump this week, while the euro and yen faced pressure as a result (Reuters).

Economic data have taken a backseat to geopolitical risks. In the US, new claims for unemployment benefits were unchanged last week and layoffs fell sharply in February. The focus is now on Friday’s employment report, with nonfarm payrolls expected to have increased by 59,000 jobs and the unemployment rate likely steady at 4.3%.

Traders have pushed back expectations for the next Federal Reserve rate easing to September or October. Similarly, Bank of England rate-cut expectations have been pared back, while bets on European Central Bank rate hikes have increased.

Currency strategists noted that the dollar’s upside is expected to persist as long as risk premia remain elevated in crude oil markets, though adjustments in positions are possible given the current risk-off tone (Reuters).

The Australian dollar rose 0.16% to US$0.701 and the New Zealand dollar gained 0.15% to US$0.5903. In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin fell 0.26% to US$70,956.52, while ether declined 0.27% to US$2,074.84.

This dollar strength reflects broader market caution as the conflict in the Middle East continues to reshape expectations for inflation, central bank actions and global financial markets.

Reuters

data.title: Technical Setup Remains Bearish For HSIF
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained a negative trading bias on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), recommending traders keep their short positions as the index continues to trade below key technical levels despite a brief rebound.

The research house said HSIF moved sideways on Thursday, gaining 55 points to close at 25,187 points. The index opened the session at 25,160 points and fluctuated between a high of 25,674 points and a low of 25,160 points before settling at the closing level. In the evening session, it retraced by 150 points and was last traded at 25,037 points.

RHB Research noted that while selling pressure appears to have moderated, the overall technical setup remains bearish as the index is still trading below both the 20-day and 50-day simple moving average (SMA) lines.

The research house expects strong resistance to emerge at 26,600 points. As long as the index remains below this level, negative momentum could pick up again and push HSIF towards the 24,500-point support level.

A decisive break below 24,500 points may extend the index’s decline further to 23,500 points.

RHB Research advised traders to retain the short position initiated at the close of Feb 26 at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss threshold set at 28,600 points to manage trading risks.

The first support level is seen at 24,500 points, followed by 23,500 points, while immediate resistance stands at 26,600 points, with the next level at 27,500 points.

data.title: Wall Street Falls As Oil Surge Fuels Inflation Fears
data.description:
US stocks closed sharply lower on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling 784.67 points or 1.61% to 47,954.74. The S&P 500 slipped 0.56% to 6,830.71 while the Nasdaq Composite eased 0.26% to 22,748.99 as escalating tensions in the Middle East rattled investors.

The conflict entered its sixth day, raising fears that fighting could spread across the region and threaten energy supplies passing through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil shipping route. Missile and drone threats have already reduced tanker traffic in the area, fuelling concerns over supply disruptions.

Oil prices surged in response. US crude jumped 8.5% to US$81 per barrel, its highest level since July 2024, while Brent crude climbed 4.9% to US$85.41. Investors worry that prolonged supply disruptions could drive inflation higher and slow economic growth.

“Look at oil today, it tells you everything you need to know about why the stock market’s down,” said Michael Antonelli, market strategist at Baird Private Wealth Management. “The market is really trying to grapple with how long this conflict will last.”

Losses were most pronounced in cyclical sectors. Industrial, materials and healthcare stocks within the S&P 500 each dropped more than 2%. Airline shares also suffered, with the passenger airlines sub-sector tumbling 5.4% and Southwest Airlines falling 6.9%.

Energy and technology counters provided some cushion. The S&P 500 energy index rose 0.6% as higher oil prices boosted revenue expectations, while Chevron gained 3.9%. Technology stocks also edged higher, supported by chipmaker Broadcom which surged 4.8% after projecting artificial intelligence chip revenue would exceed US$100 billion next year.

Despite the latest sell-off, Wall Street has still outperformed European and Asian markets this week, largely supported by technology stocks that had earlier been hit during February’s market pullback. The Nasdaq remains up 0.36% since the conflict began.

Economic data also drew attention ahead of the closely watched payroll report. New US unemployment benefit applications were unchanged last week, while stronger-than-expected ISM manufacturing and services data raised expectations for a resilient labour market.

“People are looking at the payroll numbers for tomorrow. Data (today) suggests maybe the labor market is still better than expected,” said Steve Ricchiuto, chief economist at Mizuho Securities. “But after today’s selloff, I am less convinced that it’s going to have the impact that I thought it would have. The market already pre-discounted it.”

Markets are now pricing in roughly 40 basis points of Federal Reserve rate cuts this year, down from about 50 basis points before the conflict began, according to LSEG data.

Trading activity was heavy, with volume on US exchanges reaching 22.32 billion shares compared with the 17.82 billion average over the past 20 trading days.

Reuters

data.title: Singapore Stocks Close Flat As Cautious Calm Returns
data.description:
Singapore equities finished almost unchanged on March 6 with the Straits Times Index (STI) eking out a marginal gain of 0.03 % to 4,848.25 amid a “tense calm” across Asia’s markets, even as geopolitical risks persist.

The broader iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index outperformed, climbing 0.7 % to 1,450.97, driven by selective strength in key mid‑caps.

Market sentiment was weighed down by lingering Middle East uncertainty and pressure on energy prices.

For the week, the STI slid about 2.9 %, extending its recent consolidation as investors balanced risk assets against safe‑haven flows.

Trading activity saw winners outnumber losers on the broader market, with turnover reflecting steady participation despite caution.

Investors will watch upcoming regional and global catalysts as risk sentiment continues to guide flows in and out of Singapore’s stock market.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Rally As Tech Leads Market Higher
data.description:
Hong Kong equities closed strongly on March 6 with the Hang Seng Index jumping 1.72 % to 25,757.29, buoyed by broad gains across sectors and a tech‑led rally.

Technology stocks outperformed, lifting the Hang Seng Tech Index by about 3.15 %, as optimism returned to risk assets after recent volatility.

The mainland‑linked Hang Seng China Enterprises Index also climbed 2.09 %, reflecting renewed buying interest in China‑focused names.

Despite the positive finish, the benchmark still posted a weekly decline as lingering geopolitical tensions and concerns about oil price pressures weighed on sentiment through the week.

Investors are now looking ahead to key economic data and earnings catalysts next week that could shape market direction.

data.title: COMEX Gold Eyes Upside As Bullish Momentum Strengthens
data.description:
COMEX gold rose US$11 on Wednesday, closing at US$5,134.70 as bullish momentum builds, according to RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research).

The precious metal opened at US$5,110.00, touched a session low of US$5,092.80, and reached a high of US$5,218.30 before settling above its 20-day simple moving average, signalling strength among buyers.

RHB Research noted that the gold market is positioned to resume its upward movement towards the US$5,500 level. In the event of renewed selling pressure, the commodity could retest the US$5,000 support, with US$4,800 marked as the stop-loss threshold for long positions initiated on February 11 at US$5,098.50.

Immediate resistance is anticipated at US$5,500, followed by US$6,000, while support levels are cited at US$5,000 and US$4,800.

The research house maintained a positive trading bias, advising investors to hold existing long positions while monitoring potential market fluctuations.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Closes Higher After Tech Index Rose 3%
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks gained 468.56 points, or 1.85 percent, to close Friday’s morning session at 25,789.90 points, with the tech index rising over 3 percent.

The Hang Seng Tech Index, which represents the 30 largest technology companies listed in Hong Kong, rose 3.70 percent by midday.
